rationale: Re-verified at 07eb467e. The whole round-2->round-3 diff is the bead-unique doc-target reshuffle plus command rewrites, and it is scope- and ownership-neutral. The frontmatter change (B2 workflow/overview, B3 workflow/interfaces + core/overview, B4 workflow/runbook) only moves WHICH doc file inside an already-owned domain receives the dated note; every bead's SOURCE file set is byte-identical to round-2, so domain derivation is unchanged (B1->all four, B2->workflow, B3->workflow+core, B4->workflow). Requirement/AC ownership is unchanged: R1->B1(+riders B2/B3), R2/R3/R4->B2, R5/R6->B3, R7/R8->B4; the AC1-AC9->bead map is byte-identical in ownership (only the 'Verified By' wording moved to name PASS-line checks). No AC re-assigned, no requirement dropped, no source added/removed, no new deferral. Empirically confirmed the 8 bead-owned doc paths are all unique across the four chunks (scoped uniq -d on work_chunks is empty) and every target file exists. The command rewrites (PASS-line pattern, grep -qxF exact-path doc-sync, awk section-body fence extraction, deadcode tool-run/grep split, harness carve-out drop) are the R4/R5/R6 robustness asks and tighten falsifiability without touching scope. My round-2 APPROVE holds.

findings.area: round-3 delta is scope- and ownership-neutral
findings.note: git diff 93143d92..07eb467e on plan.md = (1) frontmatter doc-target reshuffle B2 overview / B3 interfaces+core-overview / B4 runbook (B1 unchanged: four architecture.md), (2) Testing-Strategy prose rewritten to 'bead-unique domain-doc targets' + harness-seams-live note, (3) per-bead Steps/Verification reworded to the new doc target + exact-path grep, (4) provenance rounds-1/2 recap + AC-map 'Verified By' wording. No source key_file_path changed, no AC re-assigned, no Step added/removed. Deferred paths (internal/trace, .golangci.yml) still named do-not-touch in B1 s4.

findings.area: my round-2 cross-lane observation is now RESOLVED
findings.note: In round-2 I flagged that all four beads appended to workflow/architecture.md (a cross-bead doc-file touch). The R6 reshuffle removes exactly that: workflow docs are now B1 architecture / B2 overview / B3 interfaces / B4 runbook, and core is B1 architecture / B3 overview. Scoped `uniq -d` on the work_chunks doc paths is empty -> zero cross-bead doc overlap. depends_on:[] on all four now stands with no residual end-of-file append-merge coupling. Provenance 'no cross-bead source OR doc overlap' is now literally true.

findings.area: doc targets are real files (no invented docs)
findings.note: Verified all 8 reshuffled targets exist on disk: workflow/{architecture,overview,interfaces,runbook}.md, core/{architecture,overview}.md, execution/architecture.md, context-system/architecture.md. The reshuffle points at existing domain docs, so each remains a mechanical doc-sync-gate touch, not new scope.

The named per-agent tests TestManagedDocContent_{Claude,Codex,Copilot} are the naming of the round-2-required per-agent full-equality managed-doc test (spec AC3), not additional scope. PASS-line + `func <Name>` grep guards, exact-path `grep -qxF` doc-sync, and the awk section-body fence extraction only harden the verifications against false-pass; the underlying AC targets (AC1-AC9) are unchanged.

With bead-unique doc files there is now zero shared-doc merge residue, so the sequential bead-cycle order is free of doc-append conflicts. No action needed for gate-forwardness; flagging only so R2/R6 need not re-check for cross-bead append merges.
